The A7 Trunk Road (Hawick) (20mph and 30mph Speed Limits) Order 2024
The A702 Trunk Road (Carlops) (20mph Speed Limit) Order 2024
The Companies Act 2006 (Scottish public sector companies to be audited by the Auditor General for Scotland) Order 2024
items.description: Article 2 of this Order provides that Zero Waste Scotland Limited (“the company”), being a company with a registered office in Scotland, is to have its accounts audited by the Auditor General for Scotland. Article 3 provides that the company will be exempt from the auditing of company accounts requirements of Part 16 of the Companies Act 2006.

items.title: The Wildlife Management and Muirburn (Scotland) Act 2024 (Commencement No. 2 and Saving Provision) Regulations 2024
items.description: The Bill for the Wildlife Management and Muirburn (Scotland) Act 2024 (“the Act”) received Royal Assent on 30 April 2024. Sections 35 and 36 of the Act came into force the following day. These Regulations commence sections 11, 33 and 34 on 17 October 2024, and section 6 on 25 November 2024.

items.title: The Wildlife Management (Consequential Amendments) (Scotland) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations make consequential provision in connection with the commencement of section 6 of the Wildlife Management and Muirburn (Scotland) Act 2024 (“the 2024 Act”).

items.title: The Environmental Protection (Single-use Vapes) (Scotland) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations prohibit the supply, whether by sale or otherwise, in the course of business of single-use vapes and come into force on 1 April 2025.

items.title: The A82 Trunk Road (Spean Bridge) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) Order 2024
The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (West Dunbartonshire Council) Designation Order 2024
items.description: This Order applies, to the West Dunbartonshire local government area, arrangements for enforcing parking controls already available in London and certain other areas in England and in Edinburgh, Glasgow, Perth and Kinross, Aberdeen, Dundee, South Lanarkshire, Renfrewshire, East Ayrshire, South Ayrshire, East Renfrewshire, Fife, East Dunbartonshire, Argyll and Bute, Inverclyde, Highland, East Lothian, Angus, Stirling, North Lanarkshire, Midlothian, Falkirk and North Ayrshire local government areas.

items.title: The Parking Attendants (Wearing of Uniforms) (West Dunbartonshire Council) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations prescribe functions during the exercise of which a parking attendant must wear such uniform as the Scottish Ministers may determine (regulation 2).

items.title: The National Health Service Superannuation and Pension Schemes (Miscellaneous Amendment) (Scotland) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations further amend the National Health Service Superannuation Scheme (Scotland) Regulations 2011 (“the 2011 Regulations”), the National Health Service Superannuation Scheme (Scotland) (2008 Section) Regulations 2013 (“the 2013 Regulations”), the National Health Service Pension Scheme (Scotland) 2015 Regulations (“the 2015 Regulations”), the National Health Service Pension Scheme (Transitional and Consequential Provisions) (Scotland) Regulations 2015 (“the 2015 Transitional Regulations”) and the National Health Service Pension Scheme (Scotland) (Additional Voluntary Contributions) Regulations 2018 (“the 2018 Regulations”) and make temporary modifications of restrictions on return to work provisions in the 2011 Regulations, the 2013 Regulations and the 2015 Regulations and temporary modifications of member contribution provisions in the 2015 Regulations.

items.title: The Title Conditions (Scotland) Act 2003 (Rural Housing Bodies) Amendment Order 2024
items.description: This Order amends the list of prescribed rural housing bodies in the Title Conditions (Scotland) Act 2003 (Rural Housing Bodies) Order 2004 to include Shapinsay Development Trust and Tayvallich Initiative Ltd. Rural housing bodies are bodies in favour of which it is competent to create a rural housing burden comprising a right of pre-emption under section 43(1) of the Title Conditions (Scotland) Act 2003.

items.title: The Property Factors (Registration) (Scotland) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations prescribe additional information that a person must provide, and make provision for the fees that a person must pay, in relation to an application for entry in the register of property factors in terms of section 3(1) of the Property Factors (Scotland) Act 2011 (“the 2011 Act”).

items.title: The Registration of Births, Still-births, Deaths and Marriages (Prescription of Forms) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations amend the Registration of Births, Still-births, Deaths and Marriages (Prescription of Forms) (Scotland) Regulations 1997.

items.title: The A99 Trunk Road (Wick) (Temporary Prohibition on Waiting, Loading and Unloading) Order 2024
The A85 Trunk Road (Oban) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) (No. 4) Order 2024
The M90/A90 (Finavon Bridge) (Temporary Vehicle Weight Restriction) Order 2024
The A82 Trunk Road (North Ballachulish) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) Order 2024
The A85 Trunk Road (Comrie) (Temporary Prohibition on Waiting, Loading and Unloading) Order 2024
The A85 Trunk Road (Comrie) (Temporary Prohibition on Waiting, Loading and Unloading) (No. 6) Order 2024
The Registration of Births, Deaths and Marriages (Scotland) Act 1965 (Prohibition on Disposal of a Body without Authorisation) Amendment Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations amend the Registration of Births, Deaths and Marriages (Scotland) Act 1965 (Prohibition on Disposal of a Body without Authorisation) Regulations 2015. Regulation 2 makes amendments in respect of the documents required for disposal of a body in Scotland where the death occurs in England and Wales or Northern Ireland to include the coroner’s authorisation for burial or cremation where the death has not been registered.

items.title: The A85 Trunk Road (Crieff) (Temporary Prohibition on Waiting, Loading and Unloading) Order 2024
The A9 Trunk Road (Thurso) (Temporary Prohibition on Waiting, Loading and Unloading) Order 2024
The A9 Trunk Road (Tomatin to Moy) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic, Specified Turns and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) Order 2024
The North West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 10) Order 2024
The North East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 10) Order 2024
The South East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 10) Order 2024
The South West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 7) Order 2024
The A83 Trunk Road (Lochgilphead) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) Order 2024
The Good Food Nation (Scotland) Act 2022 (Commencement No. 2) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations bring into force particular sections of the Good Food Nation (Scotland) Act 2022 (“the Act”).

items.title: The M90 Trunk Road Friarton Bridge (Temporary Vehicle Weight Restriction) Order 2024
The A737 Trunk Road (Kilwinning) (Temporary Prohibition on Waiting, Loading and Unloading) Order 2024
The Town and Country Planning (Fees for Applications) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations amend the Town and Country Planning (Fees for Applications) (Scotland) Regulations 2022 (“the Fees Regulations”).

items.title: The Diligence against Earnings (Variation) (Scotland) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations make changes to certain figures relating to diligence against earnings in Part 3 of the Debtors (Scotland) Act 1987 (“the Act”).

items.title: The Plant Health (Import Inspection Fees) (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations amend the Plant Health (Import Inspection Fees) (Scotland) Regulations 2014 (the “2014 Regulations”), which makes provision relating to fees for documentary, physical and identity checks on certain plants imported into Scotland.

items.title: The Firefighters’ Pension Schemes (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Order 2024
items.description: This Order further amends the Firefighters’ Pension Scheme (Scotland) Order 2007, following amendments made to that Order by the Firefighters’ Pension Schemes (Scotland) Amendment Order 2024, extending the period during which persons who were employed in Scotland as retained firefighters have access to a pension scheme.

items.title: The A84 Trunk Road (Doune) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) Order 2024
The A84 Trunk Road (Callander) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) Order 2024
The A9 Trunk Road (Brora) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) Order 2024
The A78 Trunk Road (Inverkip) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) Order 2024
The Non-Domestic Rate (Scotland) Order 2024
items.description: This Order prescribes a rate of 49.8 pence in the pound as the non-domestic rate to be levied throughout Scotland in respect of the financial year 2024-2025. A rate of 49.8 pence in the pound was also prescribed by the Scottish Ministers as the non-domestic rate for the financial years 2022-2023 (S.S.I. 2022/36) and 2023-2024 (S.S.I. 2023/29).

items.title: The Sexual Offences Act 2003 (Prescribed Police Stations) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations amend schedule 1 of the Sexual Offences Act 2003 (Prescribed Police Stations) (Scotland) Regulations 2017 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The A985 Trunk Road (Rosyth) (Temporary 20mph Speed Restriction) Order 2024
The A7 Trunk Road (Langholm) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) Order 2024
The A82 Trunk Road (Spean Bridge) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) (No. 2) Order 2024
The A87 Trunk Road (Balmacara) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) Order 2024
The A83 Trunk Road (Arrochar) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) Order 2024
The A96 Trunk Road (Nairn) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) Order 2024
The M9/A9 Trunk Road (Thurso) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) Order 2024
The A83 Trunk Road (Campbeltown) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) Order 2024
The A96 Trunk Road (Keith) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) Order 2024
The A83 Trunk Road (Lochgilphead) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) (No. 2) Order 2024
The Dangerous Dogs (Designated Types) (Scotland) Order 2024
items.description: Article 2 of this Order designates the type of dog known as the XL Bully for the purposes of section 1 of the Dangerous Dogs Act 1991 (“the 1991 Act”).

items.title: The Council Tax Reduction (Scotland) Amendment (No. 3) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations make amendments to the Council Tax Reduction (State Pension Credit) (Scotland) Regulations 2012 (“the 2012 Regulations”) and the Council Tax Reduction (Scotland) Regulations 2021 (“the 2021 Regulations”).

items.title: The Disability Assistance (Scottish Adult Disability Living Allowance) (Consequential Amendment, Revocation and Saving Provision) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations make consequential and transitional provision in connection with the introduction of a new form of disability assistance, known as Scottish adult disability living allowance, under section 31 of the Social Security (Scotland) Act 2018 (“the 2018 Act”). Scottish adult disability living allowance is provided for by the Disability Assistance (Scottish Adult Disability Living Allowance) Regulations 2025, along with an associated form of short-term assistance under section 36 of the 2018 Act that is payable in certain circumstances when an individual’s award of Scottish adult disability living allowance is under review within the meaning of schedule 10 of that Act.

items.title: The A83 Trunk Road (Cairndow) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) Order 2024
The Protection of Vulnerable Groups (Referrals by Chief Constable) (Prescribed Information) (Scotland) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations are made under section 6A of the Protection of Vulnerable Groups (Scotland) Act 2007 (“the PVG Act”).

items.title: The Protection of Vulnerable Groups (Information for Listing and Vetting) (Scotland) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations make detailed provision in relation to information that can be used for listing and vetting purposes in respect of scheme members under the Protection of Vulnerable Groups (Scotland) Act 2007 (“the PVG Act”).

items.title: The Level 1 and Level 2 Disclosure Information (Scotland) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations make detailed provision in relation to the information which may be included in Level 1 and Level 2 disclosures under Part 1 of the Disclosure (Scotland) Act 2020 (“the Disclosure Act”).

items.title: The Consideration of Suitability for Regulated Roles (Prescribed Purposes) (Scotland) Regulations 2024
items.description: Section 68 of the Disclosure (Scotland) Act 2020 (“the Disclosure Act”) explains what is meant by the references in sections 17(1)(b) and 44(2) of the Disclosure Act to a person (“A”) considering an individual’s suitability to carry out, or to be offered or supplied for, a type of regulated role when the individual is a member of the PVG Scheme. Section 68 provides that these are references to A considering the individual’s suitability to carry out that type of regulated role for A, to be supplied by A to carry out that type of regulated role for another person, or for any other prescribed purpose.

items.title: The Disclosure and Use of Level 2 Disclosures (Prescribed Purpose and Circumstances) (Scotland) Regulations 2024
items.description: Section 42 of the Disclosure (Scotland) Act 2020 (“the Disclosure Act”) makes it an offence for a person, to whom a Level 2 disclosure is made available or disclosed, to unlawfully disclose it to another person. Section 44 of the Disclosure Act makes it an offence for a person to request, or otherwise seek sight of a Level 2 disclosure for a purpose other than a permitted purpose. Section 45 of the Disclosure Act sets out exceptions to the disclosure offences under sections 42 and 44.

items.title: The A83 Trunk Road (Ardrishaig) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) (No. 2) Order 2024
The A85 Trunk Road (Comrie) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) (No. 5) Order 2024
The A84/A85 Trunk Road (Main Street, Callander) (Temporary Prohibition on Waiting, Loading and Unloading) Order 2024
The A702 Trunk Road (Hillend) (Prohibition on Waiting, Loading and Unloading) Order 2024
The A83 Trunk Road (Tarbert) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) (No. 2) Order 2
The Coronavirus (Recovery and Reform) (Scotland) Act 2022 (Extension of Temporary Justice Measures) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations modify the expiry dates for certain temporary justice measures which are contained in the Coronavirus (Recovery and Reform) (Scotland) Act 2022 (“the 2022 Act”).

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session 1994, Sheriff Appeal Court Rules 2021 and Ordinary Cause Rules 1993 Amendment) (Taxation of Judicial Expenses) 2024
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the Rules of the Court of Session 1994, Sheriff Appeal Court Rules 2021 and Ordinary Cause Rules 1993. The timeframe for lodging objections prior to a diet of taxation is extended from three business days to 14 calendar days.

items.title: The Official Controls (Import of High Risk Food and Feed of Non-Animal Origin) Amendment (Scotland) (No. 2) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations amend Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) 2019/1793 on the temporary increase of official controls and emergency measures governing the entry into the Union of certain goods from certain third countries (“Regulation (EU) 2019/1793”).

items.title: The Dumfries and Galloway Council (Stranraer) Harbour Revision Order 2024
items.description: This Order establishes Dumfries and Galloway Council (‘the Council’) as the harbour authority for Stranraer Harbour. It defines the harbour limits and confers powers on the Council, including powers to lease and powers as regards the making of byelaws and issuing of general and special directions. It also repeals superseded, obsolete or otherwise unnecessary statutory provision affecting the harbour.

items.title: The Food Safety (Sampling and Qualifications) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations amend legislation relating to persons who may act as a food examiner in Scotland for the purpose of, and in connection with, implementing provisions relating to the recognition of professional qualifications contained in the free trade agreement between the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland and the Swiss Confederation on Recognition of Professional Qualifications made at London on 14 June 2023.

items.title: The Building (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations amend the Building (Scotland) Regulations 2004 (the “2004 Regulations”) and come into force on 1 January 2025.

items.title: The A977/A876 Trunk Road (Kincardine) (Temporary 20mph Speed Restriction) Order 2024
The A85 Trunk Road (Oban) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) (No. 5) Order 2024
The A83 Trunk Road (Inveraray) (Temporary Prohibition of Use, Pedestrians and Waiting, Loading and Unloading) Order 2024
The Feed Additives (Authorisations) and Uses of Feed Intended for Particular Nutritional Purposes (Miscellaneous Amendment) (Scotland) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations make provision regarding the authorisation of feed additives under Regulation (EC) No 1831/2003 of the European Parliament and of the Council on additives for use in animal nutrition (“EUR 2003/1831”) and regarding use of feed for a particular nutritional purpose, in Scotland.

items.title: The A83 Trunk Road (Inveraray) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) (No. 3) Order 2024
The Public Procurement (International Trade Agreements) (Miscellaneous Amendments) (Scotland) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations amend the Public Contracts (Scotland) Regulations 2015, the Utilities Contracts (Scotland) Regulations 2016 and the Concession Contracts (Scotland) Regulations 2016 (“Scottish public procurement regulations”).

items.title: The Burial (Applications and Register) (Scotland) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations make provision regarding burials in Scotland, in particular in respect of the application process for burials and the burial register. These Regulations are made for implementation of the Burial and Cremation (Scotland) Act 2016, which makes provision to regulate burial and places duties on burial authorities accordingly.

items.title: The Burial and Cremation (Scotland) Act 2016 (Commencement No. 6 and Transitional Provisions) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations bring into force particular sections of the Burial and Cremation (Scotland) Act 2016 (“the Act”).

items.title: The Upper Tribunal for Scotland Bus Registration Appeals (Composition) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations make provision as to the composition of the Upper Tribunal for Scotland when dealing with appeals under section 39(5A) of the Transport (Scotland) Act 2001 and appeals under the Public Service Vehicles (Registration of Local Services) (Bus Services Improvement Partnerships Service Standards Decisions) (Appeals) (Scotland) Regulations 2024.

items.title: The Upper Tribunal for Scotland (Transfer of Functions of the Transport Tribunal) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the transfer of certain functions of the Transport Tribunal to the Upper Tribunal for Scotland.

items.title: The Public Procurement (Miscellaneous Amendment) (Scotland) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations amend the Public Contracts (Scotland) Regulations 2015, the Utilities Contracts (Scotland) Regulations 2016 and the Concession Contracts (Scotland) Regulations 2016 to remove references to “Retained Treaties” and “retained EU law” in consequence of section 2 of the Retained EU Law (Revocation and Reform) Act 2023.

items.title: The A90 Trunk Road (Stirling to Invernettie Roundabout) (Temporary 40mph Speed Restriction) Order 2024
The A9 and A95 Trunk Roads (Dalraddy to Slochd) (Trunking) Order 2024
The A9 and A95 Trunk Roads (Dalraddy to Slochd) (Side Roads) Order 2024
The Rural Development (Continuation of Operation) (Miscellaneous Amendment) (Scotland) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations make amendments for securing the continued operation of assimilated law governing rural development in Scotland by adjusting operational end dates in relevant provisions. Regulation 2(4) also modifies the Less Favoured Area Support Scheme (Scotland) Regulations 2010 to set the payment rates for the further years of operation of that scheme.

items.title: The A9 Trunk Road (Helmsdale) (Temporary 20mph Speed Restriction) Order 2024
The A9 Trunk Road (Golspie) (Temporary 20mph Speed Restriction) Order 2024
The A83 Trunk Road (Campbeltown) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) (No. 2) Order 2024
The M9/A9 Trunk Road (Thurso and Scrabster) (Temporary 20mph, 30mph and 40mph Speed Restrictions) Order 2024
The A85 Trunk Road (Oban) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) (No. 6) Order 2024
The M9/A9 Trunk Road (Brora) (Temporary 20mph Speed Restriction) Order 2024
The Free-Range Egg Marketing Standards (Amendment) (Scotland) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations amend Commission Regulation (EC) No. 589/2008 (“EUR 2008/589”) which lays down detailed rules as regards marketing standards for eggs.

items.title: The Council Tax Reduction (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations make amendments to the Council Tax Reduction (State Pension Credit) (Scotland) Regulations 2012 (“the 2012 Regulations”) and the Council Tax Reduction (Scotland) Regulations 2021 (“the 2021 Regulations”).

items.title: The North West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Speed Restrictions) Order 2024
The Winter Heating Assistance (Pension Age) (Scotland) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations provide for winter heating assistance for eligible individuals who have reached state pension age.

items.title: The Sports Grounds and Sporting Events (Designation) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2024
items.description: This Order amends the Sports Grounds and Sporting Events (Designation) (Scotland) Order 2014 (“the 2014 Order”). The 2014 Order designates sports grounds, classes of sporting events played at those grounds and classes of sporting events outside Great Britain for the purposes of Part 2 of the Criminal Law (Consolidation) (Scotland) Act 1995 (sporting events: control of alcohol etc.).

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session 1994, Sheriff Appeal Court Rules 2021 and Sheriff Court Rules Amendment) (Statutory Interveners) 2024
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the Rules of the Court of Session 1994, the Sheriff Appeal Court Rules 2021, the Ordinary Cause Rules 1993, the Summary Application Rules 1999, the Summary Cause Rules 2002 and the Simple Procedure Rules to substitute previous, specific, provision about how certain public authorities may intervene in court proceedings using statutory intervention powers conferred on them, for general provision applicable to all statutory persons on whom intervention powers have been conferred by an enactment.

items.title: The Electronic Monitoring (Approved Devices) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2024
items.description: The Management of Offenders (Scotland) Act 2019 (“the 2019 Act”) makes provision allowing the courts when making certain disposals to require a person to submit to electronic monitoring of their compliance with a condition attached to their disposal. The Scottish Ministers can do the same for an individual being released from custody on licence. Section 8 of the 2019 Act gives the Scottish Ministers the power by regulations to prescibe “approved devices” which can be used for electronic monitoring. The types of electronic devices which are approved devices are prescribed in the Electronic Monitoring (Approved Devices) (Scotland) Regulations 2020 (“the 2020 Regulations”).

items.title: The South West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 8) Order 2024
The North West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 11) Order 2024
The North East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 11) Order 2024
The A82 Trunk Road (Fort William) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) (No. 2) Order 2024
The M77/A77 Trunk Road (Girvan) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) Order 2024
The Sandeel (Prohibition of Fishing) (Scotland) Order 2024
items.description: This Order prohibits fishing for sandeel within the Scottish zone (article 2(1)). It applies to all fishing boats, not just to Scottish or British fishing boats. The Scottish zone is defined in section 22(1) of the Sea Fish (Conservation) Act 1967 (“the 1967 Act”) by reference to a definition in section 126(1) of the Scotland Act 1998: it means the sea adjacent to Scotland up to the 200 nautical miles limit.

items.title: The South East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 11) Order 2024
The A84/A85 Trunk Road (Callander) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) Order 2024
The A83 Trunk Road (Inveraray) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) (No. 4) Order 2024
The Funeral Expense Assistance (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations amend the Funeral Expense Assistance (Scotland) Regulations 2019 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The M90/A90 (Finavon Bridge) (Temporary Vehicle Weight Restriction) (No. 2) Order 2024
The Town and Country Planning (Amendment of National Planning Framework) (Scotland) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations make provision in connection with amendment of the National Planning Framework under section 3CC(1) of the Town and Country Planning (Scotland) Act 1997 (“the Act”).

items.title: The Masterplan Consent Area Scheme (Environmental Impact Assessment) (Scotland) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations make provision in relation to assessment of the environmental impacts of certain projects where they are included within a masterplan consent area scheme made under schedule 5A of the Town and Country Planning (Scotland) Act 1997 (“the Act”).

items.title: The Land and Buildings Transaction Tax (additional amount: transactions relating to second homes etc.) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2024
items.description: This Order amends the additional amount payable in respect of residential property in relation to second homes as specified in paragraph 4(2) of schedule 2A of the Land and Buildings Transaction Tax Act 2013.

items.title: The Conservation of Salmon (Miscellaneous Amendment) (Scotland) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations amend the Conservation of Salmon (Annual Close Time and Catch and Release) (Scotland) Regulations 2014 (“the 2014 Regulations”) and the Conservation of Salmon (Scotland) Regulations 2016 (“the 2016 Regulations”).

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Fees for Applications) (Scotland) Amendment (Amendment) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations amend the Town and Country Planning (Fees for Applications) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2024 (“the 2024 Regulations”).

items.title: The Local Government Pension Scheme (Scotland) (Amendment) Regulations 2024
items.description: The Local Government Pension Scheme (Scotland) Regulations 2014 (“the 2014 Regulations”) established the Local Government Pension Scheme (Scotland) (“the Scheme”) as a career average revalued earnings scheme in accordance with section 8(2)(a) of the Public Service Pensions Act 2013 (“the Act”). The Local Government Pension Scheme (Scotland) Regulations 2018 (“the 2018 Regulations”) consolidated with certain amendments the 2014 Regulations.

items.title: The Housing (Cladding Remediation) (Scotland) Act 2024 (Commencement) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations bring into force on 6 January 2025 all of the provisions of the Housing (Cladding Remediation) (Scotland) Act 2024 (“the Act”), so far as not already in force.

items.title: The Financial Assistance for Environmental Purposes (Variation) (Scotland) Order 2024
items.description: This Order varies section 153(1) of the Environmental Protection Act 1990 to add any scheme, programme or organisation for the purposes of the protection, improvement or better understanding of air quality. This will enable the Scottish Ministers to give financial assistance to, or for the purposes of, those schemes, programmes or organisations.

items.title: The Budget (Scotland) Act 2024 Amendment Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations amend the Budget (Scotland) Act 2024 (“the Act”).

items.title: The Bankruptcy and Diligence (Scotland) Act 2024 (Commencement No. 1, Transitional and Saving Provisions) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations bring into force sections 6, 8 to 10, 13, 17, 19 and 20 of the Bankruptcy and Diligence (Scotland) Act 2024 (“the Act”) on 20 January 2025.

items.title: The Local Government Pension Scheme (Remediable Service) (Scotland) (Miscellaneous Amendment) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations make various amendments to the Local Government Pension Scheme (Transitional Provisions and Savings) (Scotland) Regulations 2014 (“the 2014 Regulations”), following their amendment by the Local Government Pension Scheme (Remediable Service) (Scotland) Regulations 2023 (“the 2023 regulations”), to correct minor errors and to align with the policy intention of the 2023 regulations. These Regulations also make various amendments to the 2023 regulations for similar purposes, and modify the Local Government Pension Scheme (Scotland) Regulations 2018 in relation to certain annual benefit statements.

items.title: The Transport (Scotland) Act 2019 (Commencement No. 8) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations appoint 26 December 2024 as the date for the coming into force of section 37 of the Transport (Scotland) Act 2019 (“the 2019 Act”) for all remaining purposes. The Transport (Scotland) Act 2019 (Commencement No. 7) Regulations 2023 previously brought section 37 into force only to provide the Scottish Ministers with the power to make regulations under inserted section 6M of the Transport Act 1985.

items.title: The Building (Procedure) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations amend the Building (Procedure) (Scotland) Regulations 2004 (the “2004 Regulations”) and come into force on 31 March 2028.

items.title: The Sexual Offences Act 2003 (Prescribed Police Stations) (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations amend schedule 1 of the Sexual Offences Act 2003 (Prescribed Police Stations) (Scotland) Regulations 2017 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Moveable Transactions (Scotland) Act 2023 (Commencement) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations bring into force on 1 April 2025 the provisions of the Moveable Transactions (Scotland) Act 2023 (asp 3) (“the 2023 Act”) which are yet to be commenced.

items.title: The Moveable Transactions (Forms) (Scotland) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the forms to be used for the purposes of sections 65(1) and 98(1) of the Moveable Transactions (Scotland) Act 2023 (asp 3) (“the Act”).

items.title: The National Health Service (Optical Charges and Payments and General Ophthalmic Services) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations amend (a) the National Health Service (Optical Charges and Payments) (Scotland) Regulations 1998 (“the 1998 Regulations”) which provide for payments to be made by means of a voucher system in respect of costs incurred by certain categories of persons in connection with the supply, replacement and repair of optical appliances and (b) the National Health Service (General Ophthalmic Services) (Scotland) Regulations 2006 (“the 2006 Regulations”) on the provision of general ophthalmic services under the National Health Service in Scotland.

items.title: The Rural Support (Improvement) (Miscellaneous Amendment) (Scotland) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations make modifications to improve the operation of the provisions of the Common Agricultural Policy (Cross-Compliance) (Scotland) Regulations 2014 (“2014 Regulations”) and the Common Agricultural Policy (Direct Payments etc.) (Scotland) Regulations 2015 (“2015 Regulations”).

items.title: The Moveable Transactions (Register of Assignations and Register of Statutory Pledges Rules) (Scotland) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations make rules for the Register of Assignations (“RoA”) and Register of Statutory Pledges (“RSP”) as established under the Moveable Transactions (Scotland) Act 2023 (“the Act”) which provide for the making up and keeping up of the registers, procedures in relation to application for registration and correction, the form and content of any document or information to be used in relation to the registers and other matters under the Act.

items.title: The Ardersier Port Limited (Pilotage Powers) Order 2024
items.description: This Order provides that Ardersier Port Limited is a competent harbour authority for the purposes of the Pilotage Act 1987.

items.title: The A85 Trunk Road (Comrie) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) (No. 6) Order 2024
The South East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 12) Order 2024
The South West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 9) Order 2024
The Bread and Flour Amendment (Scotland) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations amend the Bread and Flour Regulations 1998 (S.I. 1998/141) (“the 1998 Regulations”).

items.title: The A9 and A86 Trunk Roads (Crubenmore to Kincraig) (Trunking) Order 2024
The A9 and A86 Trunk Roads (Crubenmore to Kincraig) (Side Roads) Order 2024
The Ethical Standards in Public Life etc. (Scotland) Act 2000 (Codes of Conduct for Members of certain Scottish Public Authorities) Amendment Order 2024
items.description: This Order inserts the National Smart Ticketing Advisory Board into the list of public authorities in the schedule of the Ethical Standards in Public Life etc. (Scotland) Act 2000 (Codes of Conduct for Members of certain Scottish Public Authorities) Order 2006. The effect of this amendment is to require the Scottish Ministers to issue a code or codes of conduct in respect of the conduct of members of the National Smart Ticketing Advisory Board.

items.title: The North West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 12) Order 2024
The North East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 12) Order 2024
The Regulated Roles (Prohibitions and Requirements) (Scotland) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations make provision in relation to the removal of individuals from regulated roles in certain circumstances.

items.title: The A9 Trunk Road (Brora) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) Order 2024
The Non-Domestic Rates (Levying and Miscellaneous Amendment) (Scotland) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the amount payable as non-domestic rates in certain circumstances in respect of certain non-domestic properties in Scotland. The non-domestic rate for properties not covered by these Regulations is fixed by Order made under the Local Government (Scotland) Act 1975. For the financial year 2024-2025, the rate is fixed by the Non-Domestic Rate (Scotland) Order 2024 (S.S.I. 2024/3).

items.title: The Education (Scotland) Act 1980 (Modification) Regulations 2024
items.description: Section 53(2) of the Education (Scotland) Act 1980 (“the 1980 Act”) provides that an education authority must provide or secure the provision of a school lunch (as defined in section 53(12)) free of charge to pupils falling within section 53(7).

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Fees of Messengers-at-Arms and Sheriff Officers) (Amendment) 2024
items.description: This Act of Sederunt makes changes to the Tables of Fees regulating the fees of Messengers-at-Arms and sheriff officers.

items.title: The Packaging Waste (Data Reporting) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2024 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Packaging Waste (Data Reporting) (Scotland) Regulations 2023 (the “principal Regulations”).

items.title: The National Health Service (Common Staffing Method) (Scotland) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations make provision in relation to the common staffing method, introduced into the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978 (“1978 Act”) by section 4 of the Health and Care (Staffing) (Scotland) Act 2019 (“2019 Act”).

items.title: The Personal Injuries (NHS Charges) (Amounts) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations amend the Personal Injuries (NHS Charges) (Amounts) (Scotland) Regulations 2006 (“the principal Regulations”) which make provision in relation to the amount of NHS charges which can be recovered from a person who pays compensation to an injured person where that injured person has received National Health Service treatment or ambulance services.

items.title: The Social Care and Social Work Improvement Scotland (Cancellation of Registration and Relevant Requirements) Order 2024
items.description: This Order makes provision further to the commencement of sections 7 and 8 of the Health and Care (Staffing) (Scotland) Act 2019 (“2019 Act”) and the revocation of regulation 15 of the Social Care and Social Work Improvement Scotland (Requirements for Care Services) Regulations 2011 (S.S.I. 2011/210) under section 13(2) of the 2019 Act.

items.title: The Building (Fees) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations amend the Building (Fees) (Scotland) Regulations 2004 (“the Fees Regulations”).

items.title: The A68 (Lauder and Jedburgh) A6091/A7 (Selkirk and Hawick) A702 (West Linton and Carlops) Trunk Roads (Temporary 20mph Speed Restriction) Order 2024
The Bankruptcy (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations amend the prescribed rate of interest that applies to a claim in sequestration for the purposes of section 129(10) of the Bankruptcy (Scotland) Act 2016. The Regulations amend regulation 26 of the Bankruptcy (Scotland) Regulations 2016, which is where the existing rate of interest is specified. The new rate of interest is the Bank of England base rate which applies on the date of sequestration, plus an additional 2%.

items.title: The A77 Trunk Road (Dalrymple Street, Girvan) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) Order 2024
The Non-Domestic Rates (Transitional Relief) (Scotland) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations make provision as to the amount payable in certain circumstances as non-domestic rates in Scotland. They apply to the financial year 2024-2025. The Regulations do not apply where a non-domestic property is shown in a merged, split or reorganised entry in the valuation roll which takes effect during the financial year 2024-2025.

items.title: The A77 Trunk Road (Ballantrae) (Temporary Prohibition on Waiting, Loading and Unloading) Order 2024
The A82 Trunk Road (Spean Bridge) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) Order 2024
The A701 Trunk Road (Heathhall) (Temporary Prohibition on Waiting, Loading and Unloading) Order 2024
The A9/A99 Trunk Road (Latheron) (Temporary 40mph Speed Restriction) Order 2024

The M8 (Newhouse to Easterhouse) M73 (Maryville to Mollinsburn) A8 (Newhouse to Bargeddie) A725 (Shawhead to Whistleberry) Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) Order 2024
The Non-Domestic Rates (Islands and Remote Areas Hospitality Relief) (Scotland) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations make provision for relief from liability to non-domestic rates for lands and heritages located on islands in Scottish waters and in certain remote areas in Scotland, and operating in specified areas of the hospitality sector, for the financial year 2024-25.

items.title: The Regulation of Care (Social Service Workers) (Scotland) Order 2024
items.description: Section 44(1) of the Regulation of Care (Scotland) Act 2001 (“the Act”) requires the Scottish Social Services Council to maintain a register of social workers, social service workers of any other description prescribed and persons participating in a course or employed in a position probationary to becoming either a social worker or a social service worker of a qualified description.

items.title: The Social Security (Scotland) Act 2018 (Commencement No. 10 and Transitional Provision) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations bring sections 88, 89, 90, 91, 92, and 93 of the Social Security (Scotland) Act 2018 (“the 2018 Act”) into force on 1 April 2024 and make transitional provision.

items.title: The Discretionary Financial Assistance (Scotland) Revocation Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations revoke the Discretionary Financial Assistance Regulations 2001 (“the 2001 Regulations”), as they apply in Scotland. These Regulations do not affect the operation of the 2001 Regulations in England and Wales. The 2001 Regulations provide relevant authorities with a power to make discretionary housing payments by way of financial assistance and regulate the amount, manner, and procedure of this assistance.

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rates (Transitional Relief) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations make minor amendments to the Non-Domestic Rates (Transitional Relief) (Scotland) Regulations 2024 (“the 2024 Regulations”).

items.title: The Sea Fish (Prohibition on Fishing) (Firth of Clyde) Order 2024 (revoked)
items.description: This Order prohibits all methods of fishing within two specified areas of the Firth of Clyde from 14February to 30 April, during both 2024 and 2025 (articles 1(3) and 3(1)). The Order remains in force until the end of 30 April 2025 (article 1(2)).

items.title: The Scottish Landfill Tax (Standard Rate and Lower Rate) Order 2024 (revoked)
items.description: This Order specifies the standard rate and lower rate for Scottish landfill tax for disposals on or after 1 April 2024. The standard rate is £103.70 and the lower rate is £3.30. Tax is to be calculated by reference to the weight and types of material disposed of, as provided for in section 13 of the Landfill Tax (Scotland) Act 2014.

items.title: The Welfare of Farmed Animals (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations amend the Welfare of Farmed Animals (Scotland) Regulations 2010 (“the 2010 Regulations”). These Regulations insert a new sub-paragraph into regulation 7A(2) of the 2010 Regulations.

items.title: The Social Security (Gibraltar) (Iceland) (Liechtenstein) (Norway) (Further provision in respect of Scotland) Order 2024
items.description: This Order makes provision for the modification of certain legislation, so far as it relates to devolved matters, so as to give effect to the Agreement on Social Security as set out in the Exchange of Letters between His Majesty’s Government of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland and His Majesty’s Government of Gibraltar dated 25th January 2024 and 26th January 2024 respectively (“the Agreement”).

items.title: The Charities (Regulation and Administration) (Scotland) Act 2023 (Commencement No. 1, Transitional and Saving Provisions) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations bring various provisions of the Charities (Regulation and Administration) (Scotland) Act 2023 (“the Act”) into force.

items.title: The South East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 2) Order 2024
The South West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 2) Order 2024
The North West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 2) Order 2024
The North East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 2) Order 2024
The A96 Trunk Road (Inverness to Nairn (including Nairn Bypass)) (Side Roads) Order 2024
The A96 Trunk Road (Inverness to Nairn (including Nairn Bypass)) (Trunking & Detrunking) Order 2024
The M90/A90/A9000 Trunk Road (Saltoun Place, Fraserburgh) (Temporary Prohibition on Waiting, Loading and Unloading) Order 2024
The Dangerous Dogs (Compensation and Exemption Schemes) (Scotland) Order 2024
items.description: This Order makes provision in respect of dogs of the type known as the XL Bully. The type of dog known as the XL Bully (“the XL Bully Breed type”) is designated for the purposes of section 1 of the Dangerous Dogs Act 1991 (“the 1991 Act”) by article 2 of the Dangerous Dogs (Designated Types) (Scotland) Order 2024. Article 3 of that Order further provides that the day appointed under section 1(3) in respect of dogs of the XL Bully Breed type is 31 July 2024. This means that the possession or custody of a dog of the XL Bully Breed type will be prohibited after this date.

items.title: The Local Government Finance (Scotland) Order 2024
items.description: This Order–

items.title: The Bus Services Improvement Partnerships (Objections) (Scotland) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations set out the processes that operators of certain local bus services should follow to object to the making, variation or revocation of bus services improvement partnership plans and schemes by a local transport authority under the Transport (Scotland) Act 2001 (“the 2001 Act”), and for determining whether a sufficient number of those operators have objected.

items.title: The Provision of Early Learning and Childcare (Specified Children) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2024
items.description: This Order amends the Provision of Early Learning and Childcare (Specified Children) (Scotland) Order 2014 (“the 2014 Order”). The 2014 Order specifies those children who are eligible pre-school children for the purposes of section 47(2)(c)(ii) of the Children and Young People (Scotland) Act 2014 and who are therefore entitled to the mandatory amount of early learning and childcare (as defined in section 48 of that Act).

items.title: The Scottish Social Services Council (Appointments, Procedure and Access to the Register) Amendment Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations amend regulation 13(2) of the Scottish Social Services Council (Appointments, Procedure and Access to the Register) Regulations 2001 (“the 2001 Regulations”) to expand the categories of information that may be disclosed to persons accessing the register kept by the Scottish Social Services Council (“the SSSC”) under section 44 of the Regulation of Care (Scotland) Act 2001.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session 1994 Amendment) (Payment and Electronic Money Institution Special Administration) 2024
items.description: This Act of Sederunt makes amendments to the Rules of the Court of Session 1994 in consequence of the establishment of a new special administration procedure relating to payment institutions and electronic money institutions under the Payment and Electronic Money Institution Insolvency Regulations 2021.

items.title: The A9 Trunk Road (Almond Bridge) (Temporary Vehicle Width Restriction and Temporary 30mph Speed Restriction) Order 2024
The Consumer Scotland Act 2020 (Relevant Public Authorities) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations specify the public authorities that are relevant public authorities for the purposes of section 21 of the Consumer Scotland Act 2020 and therefore subject to the duty to have regard to consumer interests when making decisions of a strategic nature about how to exercise their functions. The public authorities specified are listed in the schedule of the Regulations.

items.title: The Planning (Scotland) Act 2019 (Commencement No. 13 and Saving Provisions) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations bring sections 16 and 50 of the Planning (Scotland) Act 2019 (“the Act”) fully into force on 1 April 2024. Section 62 of the Act is commenced on 1 April 2024 for the limited purpose of bringing paragraph 9 of schedule 2 into force. Sections 12 and 15 and paragraph 9 of schedule 2 of the Act are brought into force on that date for the purpose of enabling regulations to be made under the new sections 3CC(3) and (4) and 20AA(5) of the Town and Country Planning (Scotland) act 1997 (“the 1997 Act”) and under new schedule 5A of the 1997 Act (inserted respectively by section 12 and section 15(3) of the Act) and of bringing paragraph 5 of that new schedule 5A fully into force.

items.title: The Social Security Information-sharing (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations amend existing provisions regarding the sharing of information in connection with the social security functions of the Scottish Ministers that are contained in the Social Security Information-sharing (Scotland) Regulations 2021 (“the 2021 Regulations”).

items.title: The Police Pension Scheme (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations amend the Police Pension Scheme (Scotland) Regulations 2015 (“the 2015 Regulations”).

items.title: The Meat Preparations (Import Conditions) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations amend Commission Decision 2000/572/EC which lays down animal and public health and veterinary certification conditions for imports of meat preparations from third countries.

items.title: The Hate Crime and Public Order (Scotland) Act 2021 (Commencement and Transitional Provision) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations commence the provisions of the Hate Crime and Public Order (Scotland) Act 2021 (“the 2021 Act”), to the extent that they are not already in force. The Bill for the 2021 Act received Royal Assent on 23 April 2021. Sections 17, 21 and 22 came into force the following day.

items.title: The A830 Trunk Road (Glenfinnan) (Temporary Prohibition of Waiting) Order 2024
Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session 1994 Amendment) (National Security Prevention and Investigation) 2024
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends Chapter 89 (terrorism prevention and investigation measures) of the Rules of the Court of Session 1994 in consequence of the national security prevention and investigation measures that can be taken under the National Security Act 2023 (c. 32).

items.title: The A82 Trunk Road (Fort Augustus) (Temporary Prohibition on Waiting) Order 2024
The Plant Health (Export Certification) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2024
items.description: This Order is made under sections 3(1) and 4A of the Plant Health Act 1967 and makes provision in the area of plant health fees.

items.title: The Avian Influenza (Preventive Measures) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2024
items.description: This Order amends the Avian Influenza (Preventive Measures) (Scotland) Order 2007 (“the 2007 Order”), which makes provision to reduce the risk of transmission of highly pathogenic avian influenza from birds living in the wild to poultry and other captive birds.

items.title: The Cost of Living (Tenant Protection) (Scotland) Act 2022 (Expiry of Section 10: Extension) Regulations 2024
items.description: These regulations modify the Cost of Living (Tenant Protection) (Scotland) Act 2022 (“the 2022 Act”) to change the date when section 10 and, by consequence, schedule 3 of the Act expire.

items.title: The Rent Adjudication (Temporary Modifications) (Scotland) Regulations 2024 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations change how rent in relation to a private residential tenancy is determined on referral by a tenant to a rent officer (or on subsequent appeal to the First-tier Tribunal for Scotland) under the Private Housing (Tenancies) (Scotland) 2016 Act (“the 2016 Act”). They also change how rent in relation to statutory assured tenancies is determined under section 25 of the Housing (Scotland) Act 1988 (“the 1988 Act”) on referral to the Tribunal.

items.title: The M80 and M8 Trunk Roads (Monkland Canal Pipeline Access) (Temporary 40mph Speed Restriction) Order 2024
The Local Authority (Capital Finance and Accounting) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations make amendments to regulation 14 of the Local Authority (Capital Finance and Accounting) (Scotland) Regulations 2016 (“the 2016 Regulations”) to alter the scope for local authorities to vary repayment to their loans fund of loans fund advances made prior to 1 April 2023. Under regulation 13(3) of the 2016 Regulations loans fund advances must be repaid in accordance with proper accounting practices, but that does not apply to loans fund advances made prior to 1 April 2023.

items.title: The A835 Trunk Road (Ben Wyvis) (Temporary Clearway) Order 2024
The A87 Trunk Road (Eilean Donan) (Temporary Clearway) Order 2024

The M8 (Newhouse to Easterhouse) M73 (Maryville to Mollinsburn) M74 (Daldowie to Hamilton) A8 (Newhouse to Bargeddie) A725 (Shawhead to Whistleberry) A7071 (Bellshill) Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) Order 2024
The A737 Trunk Road (Beith) (Temporary 30mph Speed Restriction) Order 2024
The North East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 3) Order 2024
The North West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 3) Order 2024
The South East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 3) Order 2024
The Renewables Obligation (Scotland) Amendment Order 2024
items.description: This Order amends the Renewables Obligation (Scotland) Order 2009 (“the 2009 Order”).

items.title: The Budget (Scotland) Act 2023 Amendment Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations amend the Budget (Scotland) Act 2023 (“the Act”).

items.title: Bishop's Stortford Cemetery Act 2024
items.description: An Act to confer powers upon Bishop's Stortford Town Council to extinguish rights of burial and disturb human remains in Bishop's Stortford New Cemetery and Old Cemetery for the purpose of increasing the space for interments; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Post Office (Horizon System) Compensation Act 2024
items.description: An Act to provide for the payment out of money provided by Parliament of expenditure incurred by the Secretary of State under, or in connection with, schemes or other arrangements to compensate persons affected by the Horizon system and in respect of other matters identified in legal proceedings relating to the Horizon system.

items.title: Automated Vehicles Act 2024
items.description: An Act to regulate the use of automated vehicles on roads and in other public places; and to make other provision in relation to vehicle automation.

items.title: Animal Welfare (Livestock Exports) Act 2024
items.description: An Act to prohibit the export of certain livestock from Great Britain for slaughter.

items.title: Finance (No. 2) Act 2024
items.description: An Act to make provision in connection with finance.

items.title: Digital Markets, Competition and Consumers Act 2024
items.description: An Act to provide for the regulation of competition in digital markets; to amend the Competition Act 1998 and the Enterprise Act 2002 and to make other provision about competition law; to make provision relating to the protection of consumer rights and to confer further such rights; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Post Office (Horizon System) Offences Act 2024
items.description: An Act to provide for the quashing of convictions in England and Wales and Northern Ireland for certain offences alleged to have been committed while the Horizon system was in use by the Post Office; to make provision about the deletion of cautions given in England and Wales or Northern Ireland for such offences; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Media Act 2024
items.description: An Act to make provision about public service television; about the sustainability of, and programme-making by, C4C; about the name, remit, powers, governance and audit of S4C; about the regulation of television selection services; about the regulation of on-demand programme services; about the regulation of radio services; about the regulation of radio selection services; for the repeal of section 40 of the Crime and Courts Act 2013; for addressing deficiencies in broadcasting legislation arising from the withdrawal of the United Kingdom from the European Union; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Pet Abduction Act 2024
items.description: An Act to create offences of dog abduction and cat abduction and to confer a power to make corresponding provision relating to the abduction of other animals commonly kept as pets.

items.title: Paternity Leave (Bereavement) Act 2024
items.description: An Act to make provision about paternity leave in cases where a mother, or a person with whom a child is placed or expected to be placed for adoption, dies.

items.title: Building Societies Act 1986 (Amendment) Act 2024
items.description: An Act to make provision about funding of building societies and the assimilation of the law relating to companies and the law relating to building societies.

items.title: British Nationality (Irish Citizens) Act 2024
items.description: An Act to make provision for Irish citizens who have been resident in the United Kingdom for five years to be entitled to British citizenship; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Northern Ireland (Executive Formation) Act 2024
items.description: An Act to make provision to extend the period following the Northern Ireland Assembly election of 5 May 2022 during which Ministers may be appointed.

items.title: Zoological Society of London (Leases) Act 2024
items.description: An Act to amend the Crown Estate Act 1961 to increase the maximum term of the lease that may be granted to the Zoological Society of London in respect of land in Regent’s Park.

items.title: Victims and Prisoners Act 2024
items.description: An Act to make provision about victims of criminal conduct and others affected by criminal conduct; about the appointment and functions of advocates for victims of major incidents; for an infected blood compensation scheme; about the release of prisoners; about the membership and functions of the Parole Board; to prohibit certain prisoners from forming a marriage or civil partnership; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Leasehold and Freehold Reform Act 2024
items.description: An Act to prohibit the grant or assignment of certain new long residential leases of houses, to amend the rights of tenants under long residential leases to acquire the freeholds of their houses, to extend the leases of their houses or flats, and to collectively enfranchise or manage the buildings containing their flats, to give such tenants the right to reduce the rent payable under their leases to a peppercorn, to regulate the relationship between residential landlords and tenants, to regulate residential estate management, to regulate rentcharges and to amend the Building Safety Act 2022 in connection with the remediation of building defects and the insolvency of persons who have repairing obligations relating to certain kinds of buildings.

items.title: Supply and Appropriation (Main Estimates) Act 2024
items.description: An Act to authorise the use of resources for the year ending with 31 March 2025; to authorise both the issue of sums out of the Consolidated Fund and the application of income for that year; and to appropriate the supply authorised for that year by this Act and by the Supply and Appropriation (Anticipation and Adjustments) Act 2024.

items.title: Budget Responsibility Act 2024
items.description: An Act to impose duties on the Treasury and the Office for Budget Responsibility in respect of the announcement of fiscally significant measures.

items.title: Passenger Railway Services (Public Ownership) Act 2024
items.description: An Act to make provision for passenger railway services to be provided by public sector companies instead of by means of franchises.

items.title: Finance Act 2024
items.description: An Act to make provision in connection with finance.

items.title: Supply and Appropriation (Anticipation and Adjustments) Act 2024
items.description: An Act to authorise the use of resources for the years ending with 31 March 2023, 31 March 2024 and 31 March 2025; to authorise the issue of sums out of the Consolidated Fund for those years; and to appropriate the supply authorised by this Act for the years ending with 31 March 2023 and 31 March 2024.

items.title: National Insurance Contributions (Reduction in Rates) Act 2024
items.description: An Act to make provision for and in connection with reducing the main rates of primary Class 1 national insurance contributions and Class 4 national insurance contributions.

items.title: Trade (Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership) Act 2024
items.description: An Act to enable the implementation of, and the making of other provision in connection with, the Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership.

items.title: Pedicabs (London) Act 2024
items.description: An Act to make provision for regulating pedicabs in public places in Greater London; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Safety of Rwanda (Asylum and Immigration) Act 2024 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to make provision about the removal of certain migrants to the Republic of Rwanda.

items.title: Investigatory Powers (Amendment) Act 2024
items.description: An Act to amend the Investigatory Powers Act 2016; to make provision about information supplied by, or relating to, the Judicial Commissioners; and for connected purposes.

items.title: The Online Safety (List of Overseas Regulators) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations specify those overseas regulators with whom the Office of Communications (“OFCOM”) can co-operate under their power in section 114 of the Online Safety Act 2023 (c. 50) (“the Act”). “Overseas regulator” is defined in section 114(7) as “a person exercising functions in a country outside the United Kingdom which correspond to any of OFCOM’s online safety functions” (defined in section 235).

items.title: The Levelling-up and Regeneration Act 2023 (Commencement No. 5 and Transitional Provision) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations are the fifth commencement regulations bringing into force provision in the Levelling-up And Regeneration Act 2023 (c. 55) (“the Act”).

items.title: The Official Controls (Extension of Transitional Periods) and Plant Health (Frequency of Checks) (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations amend:

items.title: The Air Navigation (Restriction of Flying) (Alloa, Clackmannanshire) (Emergency) Regulations 2024
The School Teachers’ Incentive Payments (England) (Amendment) Order 2024
items.description: This Order amends the School Teachers’ Incentive Payments (England) Order 2019 (S.I. 2019/1133). The Levelling Up Premium scheme is now referred to as the Targeted Retention Incentive scheme in the list of schemes under which payment is not to be treated as remuneration for the purposes of section 122(1) of the Education Act 2002 (c. 32).

items.title: The Customs (Tariff and Miscellaneous Amendments) (No. 3) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations are made under the Taxation (Cross-border Trade) Act 2018 (c. 22).

items.title: The Financial Services Act 2012 (Relevant Functions in relation to Complaints Scheme) (Amendment) Order 2024
items.description: Section 84 of the Financial Services Act 2012 (c. 21), required the Financial Conduct Authority (“FCA”) and other financial services regulators to establish a complaints scheme, for the investigation of complaints made in connection with their exercise of, or failure to exercise, some of their functions. Article 2(h) (relevant functions of the FCA) of the Financial Services Act 2012 (Relevant Functions in relation to Complaints Scheme) Order 2014 (S.I. 2014/1195), will be amended by the Securitisation (Amendment) Regulations 2024 (S.I. 2024/705) on 1st November 2024. After this amendment made by the Securitisation (Amendment) Regulations 2024 comes into force, the amendment made by article 2 of this Order will immediately further amend article 2(h) of the Financial Services Act 2012 (Relevant Functions in relation to Complaints Scheme) Order 2014, to exclude the rule making powers that the FCA has under regulations 22 and 34 of the Securitisation Regulations 2024 (S.I. 2024/102) from the complaints scheme.

items.title: The Council Tax (Prescribed Classes of Dwellings and Consequential Amendments) (England) Regulations 2024
items.description: Section 11(2) of the Local Government Finance Act 1992 (c. 14, “the Act”) makes provision for council tax discounts of 50% in relation to empty homes. Section 11A of the Act provides for the discounts on empty homes to be reduced in relation to classes of dwellings prescribed by the Secretary of State. Section 11B of the Act was inserted by the Local Government Finance Act 2012 (c. 17) and amended by the Rating (Property in Common Occupation) and Council Tax (Empty Dwellings) Act 2018 (c. 25) and the Levelling-up and Regeneration Act 2023 (c. 55). Section 11B makes provision for billing authorities to charge a discretionary premium in relation to dwellings empty for at least one year (commonly known as an “empty homes premium”), subject to classes of exceptions prescribed by the Secretary of State. Section 11C of the Act (inserted by the Levelling-up and Regeneration Act 2023) provides for billing authorities to charge a discretionary premium on dwellings occupied periodically (commonly known as a “second homes premium”), subject to such classes of exceptions as may be prescribed by the Secretary of State under section 11D.

items.title: The Seafarers' Wages Act 2023 (Commencement) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations bring into force the remaining provisions of the Seafarers’ Wages Act 2023 (“the Act”), as anticipated by section 20(3) of the Act, insofar as they were not already in force pursuant to section 20(2) of the Act.

items.title: The Corporation Tax (Certification as Low-Budget Film) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations make provision in connection with section 1179DJA of the Corporation Tax Act 2009 (c. 4) (“the 2009 Act”), which is concerned with the provision of additional relief for low-budget films with a specified UK connection.

items.title: The Pension Protection Fund and Occupational Pension Schemes (Levy Ceiling) Order 2024 (revoked)
items.description: This Order specifies the earnings percentage used to calculate the levy ceiling (article 3) and the amount of the levy ceiling (article 4) for use in relation to the Pension Protection Fund in the financial year beginning on 1st April 2024.

items.title: The Finance (No. 2) Act 2024 (Applications for Certification as Low-Budget Film: Appointed Day) Regulations 2024
items.description: The Finance (No. 2) Act 2024 (c. 12) amended the Corporation Tax Act 2009 (c. 4) to provide for additional tax relief for low-budget films with a specified UK connection.

items.title: The Parole Board (Amendment) Rules 2024
items.description: Rule 2 of this instrument makes a number of amendments to the Parole Board Rules 2019, which set out the procedure to be adopted by the Parole Board (“the Board”) in dealing with cases for release and termination of licences.

items.title: The Pensions (Abolition of Lifetime Allowance Charge etc) (No. 2) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations amend and modify primary and secondary legislation to make further consequential provision in connection with the removal of the lifetime allowance and the lifetime allowance charge by the Finance (No. 2) Act 2023 (c. 30) and the Finance Act 2024 (c. 3).

items.title: The Payment Services (Amendment) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations amend provisions of the Payment Services Regulations 2017 which require payment service providers (PSPs) to execute payment transactions within maximum time limits (regulation 86). The amendments give a payer’s PSP the ability to delay the execution of certain payment orders where, within a specified time, the PSP establishes reasonable grounds to suspect the order has been made subsequent to fraud or dishonesty perpetrated by a third party (which may include the payee). The delay is to be used to enable the PSP to determine whether the order should be executed and must not exceed a specified time limit.

items.title: The Associated British Ports (Immingham Eastern Ro-Ro Terminal) Development Consent Order 2024
items.description: This Order authorises Associated British Ports (referred to in this Order as the undertaker) to construct, operate and maintain a new RoRo facility with three berths additional marine infrastructure, a dredged berthing pocket, and associated development within the Port of Immingham.

items.title: The Seafarers’ Wages Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations implement the Seafarers’ Wages Act 2023 (c. 8) which makes provision in relation to the remuneration of seafarers who do not qualify for the national minimum wage.

items.title: The Family Procedure (Amendment) Rules 2024
items.description: These Rules amend the Family Procedure Rules 2010 (“the FPR”) (S.I. 2010/2955).

items.title: The Architects (Fees, Electronic Communications and Miscellaneous Amendments) (Amendment) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations amend the Architects (Fees, Electronic Communications and Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 2022 (S.I. 2022/1388) in relation to certain fees chargeable by the Architects Registration Board (“the Board”). The amendments specify who is liable to pay the fees and how the fees may be paid, and require the Board to publish information about fees.

items.title: The Leasehold and Freehold Reform Act 2024 (Commencement No. 1) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations bring into force provisions of the Leasehold and Freehold Reform Act 2024 (c. 22) (“the Act”) relating to building safety.

items.title: The Levelling-up and Regeneration Act 2023 (Miscellaneous Amendment) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations are for the most part made in consequence of provision relating to the making available of childcare on domestic and non-domestic premises contained within the Levelling-up and Regeneration Act 2023 (c. 55) (“the LURA”).

items.title: The Securitisation Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations specify certain securitisation activities as “designated activities” for the purposes of the Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (see Part 5A) (c. 8) and confer powers on the Financial Conduct Authority (“FCA”) to make rules and give directions in relation to these activities. The activities are acting as an originator, sponsor, original lender or securitisation special purpose entity in a securitisation and selling a securitisation position to a retail client in the United Kingdom.

items.title: The M3 Junction 9 Development Consent (Correction) Order 2024
items.description: This Order corrects correctable errors in the M3 Junction 9 Development Consent Order 2024 S.I. 2024/752), a development consent order made under the Planning Act 2008 (c. 29), following a written request under paragraph 1(5)(a) of Schedule 4 to that Act.

items.title: The Investigatory Powers (Amendment) Act 2024 (Commencement No. 1 and Transitional Provisions) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations are the first commencement regulations made under the Investigatory Powers (Amendment) Act 2024 (c. 9) (“the 2024 Act”) which itself amends the Investigatory Powers Act 2016 (c. 25) (“the 2016 Act”). These Regulations commence most of the provisions of the 2024 Act.

items.title: The Individual Savings Account (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations amend the Individual Savings Account Regulations 1998 (S.I. 1998/1870) (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Child Trust Funds (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations amend the Child Trust Funds Regulations 2004 (S.I. 2004/1450) (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Building Societies Act 1986 (Amendment of Small Business Turnover Limit) Order 2024
items.description: This Order amends section 7(10) and (11)(c) of the Building Societies Act 1986 (c. 53) (“the 1986 Act”) by increasing the turnover limit for a small business in a relevant financial year in section 7(10) of the 1986 Act from £1,000,000 to £6,500,000 and correspondingly amending the reference in section 7(11)(c) to the equivalent amount in any other currency.

items.title: The Air Navigation (Restriction of Flying) (Alloa, Clackmannanshire) (Emergency) (Revocation) Regulations 2024
The Budget Responsibility Act 2024 (Commencement) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations bring section 1 of the Budget Responsibility Act 2024 (c. 24) (“the Act”) into force.

items.title: The Sea Fisheries (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations make amendments to assimilated law in the field of sea fisheries.

items.title: The Proceeds of Crime Act 2002 (Application of Police and Criminal Evidence Act 1984) (Amendment No. 2) Order 2024
items.description: Section 355 of the Proceeds of Crime Act 2002 (c. 29) (“POCA”) allows the Secretary of State to make an order which applies sections 15, 16, 21 and 22 of the Police and Criminal Evidence Act 1984 (c. 60) (“PACE”) with modifications, in relation to search and seizure warrants obtained in the course of certain categories of investigation in Part 8 of POCA.

items.title: The Public Interest Merger Reference (Telegraph Media Group Limited) (Pre-emptive Action) Order 2024
items.description: On 26th January 2024, the Secretary of State gave the Competition and Markets Authority and Ofcom an intervention notice on public interest grounds under section 42 of the Enterprise Act 2002 (c. 40) (“the Act”) in respect of the anticipated acquisition of the Telegraph Media Group Limited (“Telegraph Media Group”), Telegraph Media Group Holdings Limited (“Telegraph Media Group Holdings”), or the business or assets (including any shares in subsidiary undertakings) of Telegraph Media Group or Telegraph Media Group Holdings by RB Investco Limited, and/or any of its parent undertakings, subsidiary undertakings or interconnected undertakings or any of its assignees, transferees or successors in title (“the 2024 intervention notice”). An intervention notice on public interest grounds was also given to the Competition and Markets Authority and Ofcom on 30th November 2023, in respect of an associated merger, namely the anticipated acquisition of the Telegraph Media Group by RedBird IMI Media Joint Venture LLC (“the 2023 intervention notice”).

items.title: The Access to the Countryside (Coastal Margin) (Shotley Gate to Felixstowe) (No. 2) Order 2024
The HyNet Carbon Dioxide Pipeline (Correction) Order 2024
items.description: This Order corrects errors in the HyNet Carbon Dioxide Pipeline Order 2024 (S.I. 2024/436), a development consent order made under the Planning Act 2008, following a request made under paragraph 1(5)(a) of Schedule 4 to that Act.

items.title: The Armed Forces Act 2016 (Commencement No. 5) Regulations 2024
items.description: This is the fifth set of commencement regulations made under the Armed Forces Act 2016 (c. 21) (“the Act”). They bring sections 7 to 9 and 12 of the Act into force on the day after the day on which these Regulations are made.

items.title: The Media Act 2024 (Commencement No. 2 and Transitional and Saving Provisions) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations bring into force on 17th October 2024 provisions of Parts 1 and 5 of the Media Act 2024 (c. 15) (“the Act”). These are the second set of commencement regulations under the Act.

items.title: The Diocese of Ely (Educational Endowments) (The Old Parochial School) Order 2024
The Designation of Special Tax Sites (Celtic Freeport) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations designate areas, known as “special tax sites”, as special areas for the purposes of Parts 2 (plant and machinery allowances) and 2A (structures and buildings allowances) of the Capital Allowances Act 2001 (c. 2) (“CAA 2001”).

items.title: The Armed Forces (Court Martial) (Amendment) Rules 2024
items.description: These Rules amend the Armed Forces (Court Martial) Rules 2009 (S.I. 2009/2041). The Rules make provision in respect of sentence review proceedings for a service offence further to section 304D (review of sentence following offer of assistance) and section 304E (review of sentence following failure to assist) of the Armed Forces Act 2006 (c. 52). Sections 304D and 304E were inserted into the Armed Forces Act 2006 by the Armed Forces Act 2016 (c. 21).

items.title: The School Teachers' Pay and Conditions (England) Order 2024 (revoked)
items.description: This Order makes provision for the determination of the remuneration of school teachers (within the meaning of section 122 of the Education Act 2002) in England and other conditions of employment of school teachers which relate to their professional duties and working time.

items.title: The Air Navigation (Restriction of Flying) (Benwell, Newcastle) (Emergency) Regulations 2024
The Building Safety Act 2022 (Commencement No. 7 and Transitional Provisions) Regulations 2024
items.description: These Regulations are the seventh commencement Regulations made under the Building Safety Act 2022 (c. 30) (“the 2022 Act”).

items.title: The Magistrates’ Courts (Conversion of Cryptoassets) Rules 2024
items.description: These Rules prescribe the procedure to be followed for applications to a magistrates’ court for orders under Chapter 3F of Part 5 of the Proceeds of Crime Act 2002 (c. 29) (“POCA”).

items.title: The Magistrates’ Courts (Conversion of Terrorist Cryptoassets) Rules 2024
items.description: These Rules prescribe the procedure to be followed for applications to a magistrates’ court for orders under Part 4BD of Schedule 1 to the Anti-terrorism, Crime and Security Act 2001 (c. 24) (“ATCSA”).
